titleEn: Photos | Robots, drones and packed fields at Dutch Arable Farming Day
titleZh: 荷兰大田日：机器人与无人机亮相，精准农业技术引关注
summaryZh: 2025年5月28日，荷兰莱利斯塔德举办的大田日吸引了超1000名参观者，展示了机械与激光除草机器人、无人机施肥、精准中耕除草等前沿技术。活动由瓦赫宁根大学等机构联合组织，强调AI与自动化在作物管理中的应用，尤其聚焦于覆盖作物处理、行内除草和智能农机演示。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了AI视觉、机器人除草和无人机施肥等精准农业技术在田间的最新应用，与智慧畜牧中强调的自动化、传感器和智能决策高度相关，为养殖业向精准管理转型提供了可借鉴的技术思路。
contentFull: Photos | Robots, drones and packed fields at Dutch Arable Farming Day 01-06 | UncategorizedLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintKooiman imports the fully autonomous Farming GT hoeing machine, which combines mechanical in-row weed control with AI-powered camera technology. The machine is suitable for more than 100 crops, and a narrower version is currently under development. Photo: Mark Pasveer.The organisers of Arable Farming Day, held on 28 May in the Dutch town of Lelystad, Flevoland, look back very positively on the event. More than 1,000 visitors attended a wide range of demonstrations, field tours, presentations and exhibitor stands.On a sunny day, visitors watched a variety of field demonstrations with great interest. They saw mechanical and laser weeding robots, hoeing machines, cover crop incorporation techniques and drones spreading fertiliser. Exhibitors were pleased with the quality of the audience, as visitors arrived with clear objectives and targeted questions. There was also extensive interaction between visitors, Wageningen University & Research (WUR) researchers and other speakers during fourteen well-attended “Ask Anything” sessions. The annual event takes place at the WUR Open Field Crops site in Lelystad and is organised by Boerderij van de Toekomst, ACRRES, the National Field Lab for Precision Farming (NPPL), Boerderij magazine and TREKKER magazine.The precision cultivators operated in a lightly flail-mown cover crop. There was no risk of blockages and the soil surface remained level. However, with a front-mounted configuration, there is less scope to fit an additional implement for secondary cultivation of the loosened soil.Plenty to see6 machines were demonstrated for incorporating and reducing well-developed cover crops. For weed control, visitors could see three weeding robots: the TOR and Cropr laser weeders, and the Farming GT camera-guided hoeing machine supplied by Kooiman. Cropr originally developed its machine for weed control on chicory ridges, using hoes on the ridge sides, and is now adapting the technology for crops such as carrots, spinach, onions and lilies.7 machines demonstrated mechanical weed control. Farmers showed considerable interest in the technology, critically assessing weed control performance and discussing the machines’ operation with suppliers.Field demonstrations highlighted precision weed control through intelligent, highly accurate camera-guided in-row hoeing machines, either tractor-mounted or autonomous, a robotic gripper weeder, and the 9-metre-wide Lemken precision tine harrow working in sugar beet. The harrow features tine pressure adjustment from 100 grams to 5 kilograms via small hydraulic cylinders, allowing adaptation to varying soil conditions.The demonstrations attracted strong interest and were closely scrutinised. Visitors assessed whether cover crops had been sufficiently loosened, shortened and incorporated, while maintaining a level soil surface.Precision in-row hoe demonstrations2 arable farmers from Zeeuws-Vlaanderen were impressed by the tine harrow’s performance, noting that no beet plants were damaged. However, they admitted they would still find it nerve-racking to use it in their own sugar beet crops. As the saying goes when using a tine harrow: keep moving and do not look back. Precision in-row hoes on display included the Ullmana from AgriBiosolutions, the Invera from Einböck and the Samo in-row hoe.Also attracting attention was Botato, a chatbot and research prototype of an AI assistant linked to Farmmaps. The system can answer potato growers’ questions about issues such as crop diseases and potential yields based on crop data.The DJI Agras T50 is an agricultural drone capable of both spreading and spraying operations. The drone can be equipped with either a 50 kg hopper for granules and fertiliser or a 40-litre tank for liquid crop inputs.Drone demonstrations2 DJI Agras T50 agricultural drones were demonstrated. They can be used for spreading fertiliser and granules, sowing cover crops and catch crops, and applying foliar fertilisers and liquid fertilisers. Drones are becoming increasingly popular because they avoid crop damage, soil compaction and the spread of diseases. Sowing cover crops into standing wheat and maize is particularly attractive because it allows soil to remain undisturbed for up to 18 months.However, there are limitations. Not everyone is permitted to operate these drones. The pilot, company and drone must all hold the required certifications. In the Netherlands, crop protection products may not be applied by drone, and flights are restricted in certain areas. Operating large agricultural drones is specialist work generally carried out by contractors. Contractors typically charge between €40 and €60 per hectare, depending on application rates, area and conditions, with capacities of around 40 hectares per day for spraying or 10 tonnes per day for spreading.Co-authors: Kyara Derksen, Ronald Buitenhuis, Leo Tholhuijsen, Martin Smits and Lucas van de WeijerLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint Janet BeekmanadLEARN MORE Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
translationZh:
5月28日在荷兰弗莱福兰省莱利斯塔德举行的大田日，主办方对活动成果给予高度评价。超过1000名参观者参与了内容丰富的现场演示、田间考察、讲座和展位交流。

在晴朗的天气里，参观者兴致勃勃地观看了多种田间演示。他们看到了机械和激光除草机器人、中耕机、覆盖作物混入技术以及无人机施肥。参展商对观众的专业素养感到满意，因为参观者带着明确的目标和有针对性的问题前来。在十四场座无虚席的“有问必答”环节中，参观者与瓦赫宁根大学及研究中心的科研人员及其他演讲者进行了广泛互动。该年度活动在莱利斯塔德的瓦赫宁根大学开放大田作物试验站举行，由未来农场、ACRRES、国家精准农业田间实验室、Boerderij杂志和TREKKER杂志联合组织。

精准耕作机在轻度甩刀碎草的覆盖作物中作业。没有堵塞风险，土壤表面保持平整。然而，采用前置配置时，安装额外机具对松动的土壤进行二次耕作的余地较小。

**看点丰富**

6台机器演示了如何混入和减少茂盛的覆盖作物。在杂草防控方面，参观者看到了三款除草机器人：TOR和Cropr激光除草机，以及由Kooiman供应的Farming GT摄像头引导中耕机。Cropr最初为菊苣垄上的杂草防控开发了该机器，使用垄侧锄头，现在正将该技术应用于胡萝卜、菠菜、洋葱和百合等作物。

7台机器演示了机械除草。农民对这项技术表现出浓厚兴趣，认真评估除草效果，并与供应商讨论机器的操作。

田间演示突出了通过智能、高精度的摄像头引导行内中耕机（无论是拖拉机悬挂式还是自主式）、一款机器人抓取除草机，以及在甜菜田中作业的9米宽雷肯精密弹齿耙实现的精准杂草防控。该弹齿耙通过小型液压缸实现从100克到5公斤的弹齿压力调节，从而适应不同的土壤条件。

演示吸引了强烈关注，并受到仔细审视。参观者评估了覆盖作物是否已充分松碎、缩短并混入土壤，同时保持土壤表面平整。

**精准行内中耕演示**
author: Janet Beekman
